finding.recommendation: Create one unified governance tree under deep-loop-workflows, with one lowercase feature_catalog root and one lowercase manual_testing_playbook root, but partition both by top-level modes: context, research, review, improvement, and council. Preserve each mode's existing subtree, local IDs, category model, count rules, and cross-reference semantics; resolve CP-* collisions by mode-qualified IDs at the merged-root index level rather than renumbering files to CTX-/RES-/REV-/IMP-/COU-. Normalize council's FEATURE_CATALOG.md to feature_catalog.md, update old skill-root paths mechanically while preserving deep-loop-runtime paths, and update the council anchor-integrity test to the new merged council playbook path.
key_tradeoffs: One root preserves the new single-skill governance convention, while mode subtrees avoid false category unification.
Mode-qualified existing IDs minimize churn and protect byte-identical behavior, but root-level readers must understand qualified IDs like research/CP-046.
Lowercase normalization aligns with runtime and other skills, but council has 32 per-feature back-links to update.
A shared sandbox setup helper reduces duplication, but mode-specific payload manifests must remain separate.
risks: Over-flattening categories would erase mode semantics and violate the migration constraints.
Global ID renumbering would create broad bidirectional catalog/playbook churn with no behavior benefit.
Existing count drift in deep-improvement and deep-loop-runtime can produce a wrong merged total if copied blindly.
Council uppercase FEATURE_CATALOG.md references can become broken links after normalization.
Broad old-path rewrites can accidentally rewrite deep-loop-runtime or historical changelog/archive paths.
depends_on: Q-CMD 5-6 command surface and orchestration entrypoints
Q-ADVISOR 13-15 aliases and merged skill metadata
Q-BACKEND 7-10 source ownership and runtime/workflow boundary
open_questions: Should PR-001 in deep-improvement Lane D be counted as shipped, making the merged playbook total 199 instead of the baseline 198? I recommend yes because the file exists and has frontmatter.
Will the final layout use modes/<mode>/ exactly, or a different mode directory spelling?
Will agent names remain stable, allowing agent path references to stay unchanged?
